parsed_review.summary: CP00-584 is a descriptor-only RP19 (Client Portal) closeout pack covering RP19.P01.M06.S01-RP19.P02.M04.S06 (150 units, risk C). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claims are supported and appropriately scoped. (1) Coverage: registry CP584 deliverable_counts {ui:23,implementation:77,fixture:3,test:17,hermes_evidence:3,claude_review:6,contract:5,security_audit:10,failure_recovery:6} and micro_title_row_counts match the prompt baseline, the manifest delivery_counts, the README, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-584 entry (unit_count 150, range and ledger sha aligned); phase split RP19.P01:71 / RP19.P02:79 sums to 150. (2) Descriptor-only boundaries: every generated row carries descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false, and freezeCp584Result stamps writes_product_state:false, loads_real_fixture_data:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, evaluates_runtime_permission:false, plus all leak guards false; manifest sets production_ready:false and runtime_ready:false. (3) Authority boundary: claude_is_final_approval:false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false are enforced in registry attestations, the descriptor, the contract current_pack/no_write_attestation, and the unit tests; production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification label and Hermes production_ready_candidate is gated on coverage.valid && slice.valid (a candidate, not a runtime/enterprise claim). (4) Hermes usage is descriptor/control-plane only (receipt_shape '..._no_write'); no product runtime is embedded or dispatched, and source files are pure functions with read-only validation (readFile/console.log), consistent with the no-write attestation. (5) The validator deep-equals the descriptor/requirements/attestation against the committed contract and validates coverage against the live plan; command-evidence records node --check, node --test, rp19:client-portal-core:validate, closeout-pack-plan:validate, npm test, and npm run validate at exit 0. (6) Review hardening: the request is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob, forbidden_tools_present:false), prompt is 2478 bytes with no embedded diff/source, auth preflight passed (exit 0), readiness is ready, and no invalid attempts are recorded for this pack. Two non-blocking P3 transparency notes: downstream adjudication.md/construction-inspection.json are not yet present (expected — they follow this read-only review in the hardened sequence), and changed-file-scope.json dual-lists new-session-handoff.md. No P0/P1/P2 issues; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout. Final human approval remains required and is unaffected by this read-only review.

findings.title: Downstream adjudication and construction-inspection artifacts not present at read-only review stage
findings.details: The manifest lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-584/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json as required_pack_artifacts/evidence_refs, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. The review focus asks the production_ready claim to be supported by 'adjudication, inspection' evidence; those two legs cannot be inspected at this point because, per CLIENT_PORTAL_CORE_CP584_REQUIREMENTS.hardened_review_sequence, finding_adjudication and construction_inspection are produced AFTER the read_only_claude_run_with_raw_capture step (this review). command-evidence.json notes these as post-review gated commands executed and recorded before commit. This is expected sequencing, not a defect — recorded for adjudicator transparency.
findings.recommendation: No action required at review time. The closeout tooling must produce and validate claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json before final closeout-pack:validate and commit, as the manifest and command-evidence already require.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected pre-review state per hardened review sequence; non-blocking; to be satisfied by downstream adjudication/inspection/closeout steps before commit.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-lists new-session-handoff.md as both untracked implementation and unrelated preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 159) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 165). It is not one of the eight active_pack_implementation_refs and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so the pack's actual deliverable scope is unaffected. This is a minor metadata redundancy in the scope file, not a pack deliverable defect, and the reviewer instruction to not infer unrelated dirty files as part of the pack still holds.
findings.recommendation: Optionally refine the scope-generation tooling so a planning/handoff document is not simultaneously classified as untracked_implementation and unrelated_dirty; no change to CP00-584 deliverables is needed.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; non-blocking; affects scope-report tooling metadata only, not pack deliverables.
